world.headline: Iran threatens to expand war to Indian Ocean, Bab al-Mandab

main.text: BREAKING: Iran's Supreme Leader's Top Military Advisor to CNN: 'If the War continues and the Naval Blockade is not lifted we will drag the war to the Indian Ocean, the Bab al-Mandab Strait and the Mediterranean Sea. We will give another dimension to the war by attacking these other American bases...'

perspectives.text: Iran is desperate, threatening escalation because they are losing.
perspectives.note: The post accurately reports the advisor's statement from the CNN interview (primary). It adds context on the advisor's IRGC history and 'message to Trump'. Framing is 'vows to expand'; neutral reporting with interpretive context. High verifiability and primary-source proximity.

perspectives.text: US blockade and policy are provoking Iran to widen the conflict.
perspectives.note: The post reports the threat and frames it as Iran responding to US actions ('striking as they flee'). The quote is accurate, but the causal 'pushing' is interpretive without additional primary evidence in the post. Medium accuracy on the statement, lower on the 'provoking' narrative.

world.headline: Putin: Iran has not requested any weapons from Russia

main.text: JUST IN: Russian President Putin says Iran has not requested any weapons from Russia.

perspectives.text: Putin staying out to avoid direct confrontation with US.
perspectives.note: The post accurately reports Putin's statement (primary from the Russian president). Framing is straightforward news. High verifiability as direct quote from a head of state.

perspectives.text: Russia is playing both sides, not committing to Iran.
perspectives.note: The post is used here as context for the broader war, but the note is that the Putin statement shows Russia is not providing weapons, limiting escalation. The accuracy of the 'not requested' claim is from the Putin statement; framing in surrounding coverage is interpretive.

world.headline: US seizes Iranian shadow fleet tanker in Indian Ocean

main.text: US announces it has seized a stateless vessel 'MT DAVINA' belonging to the shadow fleet of Iran, in the Indian Ocean.

perspectives.text: US is enforcing sanctions and hitting Iran's oil revenue.
perspectives.note: The post reports the US action against the sanctioned Iranian tanker (primary from US forces). Framing is enforcement of sanctions. High verifiability from the official action.

perspectives.text: US is escalating the economic war on Iran in the region.
perspectives.note: The post reports the seizure as part of the shadow fleet evasion. The 'escalating' is interpretive, but the fact of the seizure is accurate from the US announcement. Medium on framing, high on the event verifiability.

nation.headline: Fetterman refuses to block Trump judicial nominee

main.text: BREAKING: Sen. John Fetterman (D-PA) has just REFUSED to block a Trump judicial nominee, despite having the unilateral ability to do so with the 'blue slip' — Democrats are FURIOUS. This is the *first time* in Trump's 2nd term a Democrat did not block a Trump nominee!

perspectives.text: Fetterman breaking with his party on Trump nominees.
perspectives.note: The post accurately reports the factual event of Fetterman not using the blue slip (verifiable from Senate procedure and the nominee advancing). 'Democrats are FURIOUS' is reported as reaction. High verifiability of the action, good primary proximity via the Senate context.

perspectives.text: Fetterman is enabling Trump to pack the courts with extremists.
perspectives.note: The post reports the 'blue groups are gearing up to run ADS against Fetterman'. The fact of the refusal is accurate, but the 'enabling extremists' is loaded framing from the reaction. Medium accuracy on the event, lower on the interpretive 'pack the courts' without the nominee's record in the post.

nation.headline: US Attorney: Multiple election fraud investigations underway in CA

main.text: BREAKING: FA US Attorney Bill Essayli reveals MULTIPLE 'election fraud investigations' are underway in California in conjunction with the FBI. 'California’s election system has serious structural vulnerabilities. Universal vote-by-mail with no voter ID requirements creates conditions where fraud can go undetected...'

perspectives.text: California's mail-in system is wide open to fraud.
perspectives.note: The post quotes the US Attorney directly from the official statement (primary). The 'wide open to fraud' is the official's conclusion based on 'universal vote-by-mail with no voter ID'. High verifiability from the govt source, good primary proximity.

perspectives.text: The investigations are needed to restore confidence in CA elections.
perspectives.note: The post includes the Attorney's commitment to 'follow the evidence wherever it leads and prosecute any violations'. The call for confidence is the stated goal in the primary statement. High accuracy and verifiability from the official announcement.

nation.headline: Trump wants Pulte to carry out mass firings in intel community

main.text: JUST IN: PRESIDENT TRUMP WANTS ACTING DNI BILL PULTE TO CARRY OUT MASS FIRINGS — WSJ. 'I think there are a LOT of people in there that shouldn't be there [in the Intel community],' Trump told WSJ. Trump says he wants @Pulte to 'start the process' of firings.

perspectives.text: Deep State panic over Pulte cleaning out the intel agencies.
perspectives.note: The post accurately reports Trump's WSJ comments on wanting mass firings in the intel community (primary via WSJ). 'Deep State panic' is interpretive framing based on the 'why the Deep State and establishment senators are panicking' context. Medium-high on the quote, lower on the 'panic' narrative without direct evidence in the post.

perspectives.text: Trump is politicizing the intelligence community with loyalist purges.
perspectives.note: The post reports the 'mass firings' goal. The 'politicizing' and 'loyalist purges' is the opposing interpretation of the same facts. The quote is accurate, but the framing is loaded without the counter view that the community needs reform. Medium accuracy on the event, lower on the one-sided 'politicizing' label.

business.headline: $1 trillion erased from stock market today

main.text: JUST IN: $1 trillion erased from stock market today

perspectives.text: Market reacting to policy uncertainty and tariff risks.
perspectives.note: The post states the $1T drop as fact (verifiable from market data). The 'policy uncertainty and tariff risks' is common interpretive context for the move. High verifiability of the number, medium on the causal framing without specific data in the post.

perspectives.text: Market volatility is the price of America First economic reset.
perspectives.note: The post is a long reasoned take on related AI/gov issues but the note is that the market drop is acknowledged in context of broader policy. The accuracy of the drop is from the Kalshi post; the 'price of reset' is opinion. Medium on the causal link.

business.headline: David Sacks: Bernie Sanders' 50% government stake in AI is a bad idea

main.text: While I’m no fan of socialism or arbitrary confiscations of wealth, I can see why Bernie Sanders’ proposal (for the government to take a 50% stake in AI companies) resonates... Nationalization of AI will accelerate the corporate-government fusion we’re already sliding toward... America won’t win the AI race if we beat China but end up with a CCP-style social credit system in the U.S.

perspectives.text: Government ownership of AI will lead to Orwellian control and lose the race to China.
perspectives.note: The post is a long, substantive argument from a tech investor against the Sanders proposal. It cites the CEOs' own statements on job loss, the PBC status, and risks of government control over information and behavior. High on verifiability (cites public statements), accuracy of the risks (historical social media censorship precedent), and caveats on both sides' fears. Excellent primary-source proximity via the CEOs' own words.

perspectives.text: AI companies have made billions from public data; the public deserves a stake in the trillions created.
perspectives.note: The post (Bernie) accurately notes that the largest AI companies trained on the collective knowledge of humanity and that trillions will be created. The '50% ownership stake' is the policy proposal to 'guarantee that the trillions created by AI are used to improve the lives of all of us'. High accuracy on the public data/training fact, medium on the 'public deserves' framing as it is a value judgment without addressing innovation incentives or implementation details.

business.headline: S&P rejects fast track for mega-caps; SpaceX waits till 2027

main.text: SpaceX will reportedly not be fast-tracked into the S&P 500, per the S&P DJI. The requirements that will now remain in place are: No changes to S&P 500 eligibility rules for mega-cap companies. Mega-cap companies will still need to wait 12 months after their IPO...

perspectives.text: S&P is preserving the integrity of the index by not making exceptions for size alone.
perspectives.note: The post accurately reports the S&P decision and the specific criteria that remain (financial viability, seasoning, IWF). It is close to the primary S&P announcement. Framing is neutral and factual. High verifiability and primary-source proximity.

perspectives.text: Bureaucracy is holding back America's most innovative company.
perspectives.note: The post calls the decision 'great news' for not fast-tracking into 'madness' and that 'SpaceX now needs to stand on its own two feet'. It accurately reflects the no-change outcome. Framing is opinion in favor of strict rules. High verifiability of the result, medium on the 'bureaucracy' critique without the full methodology rationale.
